title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is practical accountability: new work focuses on auditing whether models truly forget data, adapting governance to AI agents acting in open-source workflows, and benchmarking failure modes in high-stakes multimodal systems. The mix leans toward evaluation and governance mechanisms that could shape how capable models are supervised, audited, and trusted in deployment.

items.title: New framework for auditing machine unlearning
items.why_it_matters: Auditable unlearning is central to privacy, compliance, and verifying deletion claims in deployed models.

items.title: Regulating the Machine Contributor: Governance and Policy Alignment in Open Source
items.why_it_matters: It addresses how open-source governance must adapt when AI agents can autonomously propose and submit code changes.

items.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
items.why_it_matters: Stage-level hallucination diagnosis could improve evaluation and mitigation for medical multimodal systems.

items.title: CORA: Analyzing and bridging thinking-answer gap in Multimodal RLVR via Consistency-Oriented Reasoning Alignment
items.why_it_matters: It targets mismatches between reasoning traces and answers, a key reliability issue in multimodal alignment.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.why_it_matters: The update signals engagement with EU transparency and provenance standards for AI-generated content.

items.title: LoSoNA: A Benchmark for Local Social Norm Adaptation in Group Conversations
items.why_it_matters: Benchmarks for local norm adaptation help evaluate whether agents behave appropriately in multi-user social settings.

items.title: From Self-Supervised Speech Models to Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Anti-Spoofing
items.why_it_matters: More robust anti-spoofing improves defenses against synthetic voice misuse and authentication fraud.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
